source_jobs.title: Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Highbeam is building the future of business banking and cash management.

Our platform combines AI agents, automated financial workflows, and integrated financial products that save brands time and money.

Customers have generated billions in sales and include well-known brands such as Cuts https://www.cutsclothing.com/, Tushy https://hellotushy.com/, NYON https://newyorkornowhere.com/, Sabah https://www.sabah.am/, Still Here https://www.stillhere.nyc/, Alice Mushrooms https://alicemushrooms.com/, Original Grain https://www.originalgrain.com/, birddogs https://www.birddogs.com/, and more.

Our team includes alumni of Shopify, Square, Toast, Rippling, and McKinsey.

We’ve raised $42M in equity from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/962259dc-2fcd-40ed-8403-289c8a933cc7/image.png]



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/4192f743-4861-44cd-a1f6-473b86c927ae/join%20us%20(1).png]




ABOUT THIS ROLE



We’re looking for software engineers to join us in building the future of business banking and cash management. You’ll work closely with other engineers, product, and design to design, build, and iterate on core products used by businesses every day.

You’ll own projects end-to-end: from scoping to building, testing, shipping, and refining based on real-world feedback.

Our stack: Kotlin, TypeScript / React, PostgreSQL, Google Cloud. Check out our open-source Kotlin toolkit Kairo https://github.com/highbeamco/kairo.



On AI use

A strong Highbeam engineer uses AI as a powerful tool — for exploration, design, and implementation — while maintaining full ownership of the outcome. AI can accelerate thinking and execution, but it does not replace judgment, taste, or accountability.

We expect engineers to use AI extensively and still care deeply about code quality. That means reviewing outputs carefully, refining them, understanding the trade-offs, and ensuring the final result meets a high bar for clarity, correctness, and maintainability.

Shipping working code is not enough. You should understand what you ship and be able to defend the decisions behind it.



Qualifications

 - 4+ years of professional software engineering experience (startup or small-team experience is a plus)

 - Proven ability to lead and deliver technically challenging, cross-functional projects

 - Comfortable with designing APIs, debugging complex systems, and writing tests

 - Strong product sense and ability to make thoughtful tradeoffs in scope and functionality

 - Interest in (or willingness to learn) basic financial concepts relevant to small businesses



You'll thrive at Highbeam if...

 - You love working in-person with a high-performing team

 - You enjoy working in an idea-meritocratic, low-ego environment

 - You are proactive and self-directed, and you excel in ambiguous, fast moving environments

 - You care about delivering a polished customer experience

 - Bonus: You have experience working in fintech, AI or early-stage startups



What we offer

 - Chance to join the founding team of a well-funded startup chasing a huge opportunity

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Great location: Our NYC office is in NoHo, a short walk from the A,C,E · B,D,F,M · N,Q,R,W · 1 · 6 trains. Our Toronto office is downtown near St. Andrew Station.

 - Generous PTO

 - Comprehensive health & benefits package

 - Stipends for lunch, commute, wellness, and purchasing customer products

 - Yearly offsite—last year we went to Mexico City!

source_jobs.title: Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
Highbeam is building the future of business banking and cash management.

Our platform combines AI agents, automated financial workflows, and integrated financial products that save brands time and money.

Customers have generated billions in sales and include well-known brands such as Cuts https://www.cutsclothing.com/, Tushy https://hellotushy.com/, NYON https://newyorkornowhere.com/, Sabah https://www.sabah.am/, Still Here https://www.stillhere.nyc/, Alice Mushrooms https://alicemushrooms.com/, Original Grain https://www.originalgrain.com/, birddogs https://www.birddogs.com/, and more.

Our team includes alumni of Shopify, Square, Toast, Rippling, and McKinsey.

We’ve raised $42M in equity from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/962259dc-2fcd-40ed-8403-289c8a933cc7/image.png]



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/4192f743-4861-44cd-a1f6-473b86c927ae/join%20us%20(1).png]




ABOUT THIS ROLE



We believe great design is essential to building trusted financial products. Our customers rely on us to help them understand their cash flow, make financial decisions, and run their businesses with confidence. In this role, you’ll help design clear, intuitive interfaces for complex financial workflows - turning raw data, money movement, and financial controls into products that feel simple and reliable.

You’ll be a core contributor to the design of our fintech platform, working closely with product and engineering to shape how brand founders interact with their finances every day.



Responsibilities

 - Design end-to-end product experiences for financial workflows such as payments, financial workflows and controls

 - Translate complex financial concepts and customer needs into simple, intuitive, and trustworthy interfaces

 - Work closely with product and engineers to scope, iterate, and ship quickly

 - Collaborate with customers as needed to validate assumptions and refine designs

 - Contribute to shared design principles and patterns across the product

 - Help build a strong product and design culture through thoughtful collaboration



Qualifications

 - 4+ years of experience as a product designer, ideally on fintech, financial tools, or SMB products with a product-led growth approach

 - Comfort designing workflow-driven products; understanding (or interest in learning) of basic financial concepts as applied to SMB businesses

 - High craft and strong UX rigor with a thoughtful focus on hierarchy, interaction, and polish that turns complexity into intuitive experiences

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to collaborate across product, engineering, and business teams

 - Demonstrated ability to make thoughtful tradeoffs between clarity, usability, and scope in complex systems



You'll thrive at Highbeam if...

 - You love working in-person with a high-performing team

 - You enjoy working in an idea-meritocratic, low-ego environment

 - You are proactive and self-directed, and you excel in ambiguous, fast moving environments

 - You care about delivering a polished customer experience

 - Bonus: You have experience working in fintech, AI or early-stage startups



What we offer

 - Chance to join the founding team of a well-funded startup chasing a huge opportunity

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Great location: Our NYC office is in NoHo, a short walk from the A,C,E · B,D,F,M · N,Q,R,W · 1 · 6 trains. Our Toronto office is downtown near St. Andrew Station.

 - Generous PTO

 - Comprehensive health & benefits package

 - Stipends for lunch, commute, wellness, and purchasing customer products

 - Yearly offsite—last year we went to Mexico City!

ABOUT THIS ROLE


We're looking for a frontend engineer to help us ship features that users find both useful and delightful to use.

Our stack: Kotlin, TypeScript / React, Python, PostgreSQL, Terraform, Google Cloud.

This role will be in-person in New York or Toronto.



What you’ll do

 - Refine our existing React app and build new features

 - Be the frontend expert other engineers can rely on to help debug frontend issues

 - Work with a modern frontend stack including react, typescript, vite, storybook, react-router-dom, and radix-ui



On AI use

A strong Highbeam engineer uses AI as a powerful tool — for exploration, design, and implementation — while maintaining full ownership of the outcome. AI can accelerate thinking and execution, but it does not replace judgment, taste, or accountability.

We expect engineers to use AI extensively and still care deeply about code quality. That means reviewing outputs carefully, refining them, understanding the trade-offs, and ensuring the final result meets a high bar for clarity, correctness, and maintainability.

Shipping working code is not enough. You should understand what you ship and be able to defend the decisions behind it.



This role will be a great fit for you if...

 - You are highly proficient with TypeScript and React

 - You have 4+ years of professional experience building React apps

 - You are comfortable with rewriting code to improve code quality

 - You question requirements before building and, once set, you implement designs perfectly



You'll thrive at Highbeam if...

 - You love working in-person with a high-performing team

 - You enjoy working in an idea-meritocratic, low-ego environment

 - You are proactive and self-directed, and you excel in ambiguous, fast moving environments

 - You care about delivering a polished customer experience

 - Bonus: You have experience working in fintech, AI or early-stage startups



What we offer

 - Chance to join the founding team of a well-funded startup chasing a huge opportunity

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Great location: Our NYC office is in NoHo, a short walk from the A,C,E · B,D,F,M · N,Q,R,W · 1 · 6 trains. Our Toronto office is downtown near St. Andrew Station.

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Highbeam is building the future of business banking and cash management.

Our platform combines AI agents, automated financial workflows, and integrated financial products that save brands time and money.

Customers have generated billions in sales and include well-known brands such as Cuts https://www.cutsclothing.com/, Tushy https://hellotushy.com/, NYON https://newyorkornowhere.com/, Sabah https://www.sabah.am/, Still Here https://www.stillhere.nyc/, Alice Mushrooms https://alicemushrooms.com/, Original Grain https://www.originalgrain.com/, birddogs https://www.birddogs.com/, and more.

Our team includes alumni of Shopify, Square, Toast, Rippling, and McKinsey.

We’ve raised $42M in equity from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/962259dc-2fcd-40ed-8403-289c8a933cc7/image.png]



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/4192f743-4861-44cd-a1f6-473b86c927ae/join%20us%20(1).png]




ABOUT THIS ROLE



We are looking for an experienced data scientist to help build the analytical engine behind how we understand customer health, behaviors, product usage, success patterns, and risks across our portfolio of ecommerce brands.

This role blends data science, customer analytics, financial analysis, and market insights to answer complex questions about how brands grow, what drives profitability, and where the market is headed. You’ll work cross-functionally with Engineering, Product, CS, GTM, and Capital to explain why customers perform the way they do, and how Highbeam can better serve them.

If you love turning messy real-world data into crisp insights, building heuristics for “what good looks like,” and uncovering the drivers behind customer success, this role is for you.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

Build a deep, data-driven understanding of each customer

 - Analyze customer-level financial health, including revenue consistency, margin structure, cashflow, marketing efficiency, inventory cycles, capital usage, and risk indicators

 - Create customer health scores, segmentations, and heuristics that the entire company can rely on

 - Identify early signs of distress, churn risk, and growth potential

Build portfolio level intelligence

 - Conduct quantitative research across the entire platform and market sector to understand themes in e-commerce: revenue volatility, seasonality, acquisition dynamics, category-level differences, margin pressures, discounting trends, etc

 - Benchmark customers against peers to define “healthy” vs “unhealthy” patterns

Produce insights that guide customer-facing teams

 - Partner with CS, GTM, and Capital teams to analyze behavior across segments, identify high-value opportunities, and explain customer challenges

 - Build reporting and dashboards that reveal patterns in product usage, financial outcomes, and customer lifecycle journeys

 - Surface actionable insights to improve activation and strengthen customer relationships

Translate insights into strategy

 - Inform underwriting models with customer behavior and financial patterns

 - Partner with Product to define metrics, craft intelligence features, and identify opportunities for automation and ML

 - Enable GTM to tell more compelling customer stories grounded in data




QUALIFICATIONS

Required

 - 5+ years experience in customer analytics, data science, financial analysis, or a related quantitative field

 - Strong background in statistics, machine learning, computer science, or quantitative discipline, especially with large data sets. Comfortable working with messy real-world data is a must

 - Experience analyzing customer behavior, product usage, lifecycle metrics, or financial/transactional datasets

 - Ability to synthesize complex data into clear insights and compelling narratives for non-technical audiences

 - Familiarity with e-commerce fundamentals (conversion, CAC, LTV, margin structure,

source_jobs.title: AI Implementation Associate
source_jobs.description:
Highbeam is building the future of business banking and cash management.

Our platform combines AI agents, automated financial workflows, and integrated financial products that save brands time and money.

Customers have generated billions in sales and include well-known brands such as Cuts https://www.cutsclothing.com/, Tushy https://hellotushy.com/, NYON https://newyorkornowhere.com/, Sabah https://www.sabah.am/, Still Here https://www.stillhere.nyc/, Alice Mushrooms https://alicemushrooms.com/, Original Grain https://www.originalgrain.com/, birddogs https://www.birddogs.com/, and more.

Our team includes alumni of Shopify, Square, Toast, Rippling, and McKinsey.

We’ve raised $42M in equity from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/962259dc-2fcd-40ed-8403-289c8a933cc7/image.png]



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/4192f743-4861-44cd-a1f6-473b86c927ae/join%20us%20(1).png]




ABOUT THIS ROLE



We're hiring a Finance AI Implementation Associate to sit at the seam between our customers and our product. You'll work directly with brands to optimize their cash across cash management, bill pay, and forecast health on our agentic platform and you'll be the primary channel feeding what you learn back into what we build. Every manual workflow you run today is a candidate for tomorrow's autonomous capability, and you'll be the one spotting which ones, specifying them, and partnering with Product and Engineering to ship them.This role is based in our NoHo, NYC office.




What you’ll do

 - Implement and operate customer workflows. Configure each customer's setup across cash management recommendations, AP and bill pay, and forecast health monitoring. Own the outcome, customers should see measurable cash improvements from your work

 - Work hands-on with the agentic platform. Prompt it, evaluate outputs, tune behavior, and build the skills and workflows it uses to serve customers. You're not just a user of the platform, you're shaping how it behaves

 - Partner closely with Product. Spot patterns across customers, translate them into clear asks, and work with PMs and engineers to translate manual workflows into autonomous ones

 - Prototype new AI-powered workflows. When a customer need doesn't fit what exists, build something. Partner with customers and internal teams to test ideas quickly and bring the winners into the product

 - Solve ambiguous financial problems. Break down messy customer situations, reason through them, and design workflows that scale beyond a single brand

 - Engage customers directly. Be a person they trust to optimize their cash and resolve issues completely



Qualifications

 - Experience in a customer-facing, strategy & operations or product role

 - Strong analytical thinking; able to break down ambiguous financial problems and reason through them clearly

 - Hands-on experience tinkering with AI tools like Claude, ChatGPT, or Cursor (personal projects, school, or work all count)

 - Desire to learn; able to pick up on unfamiliar concepts quickly and directly apply in day-to-day work

 - Understanding of core financial concepts (cash flow, P&L, AP/AR) and genuine curiosity about fintech

 - Comfort in Excel or Google Sheets modeling and analysis

 - Strong communication skills; can articulate complex concepts clearly to customers

 - Comfort working cross-functionally and moving fast in ambiguous, early-stage environments

 - BA or higher, preferably in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Computer Science, or a related field



You'll thrive at Highbeam if...

 - You love working in-person with a high-performing team

 - You enjoy working in an idea-meritocratic, low-ego environment

 - You are proactive and self-directed, and you excel in ambiguous, fast moving environments

 - You care about delivering a polished customer experience

 - Bonus: You have ex

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Highbeam is building the future of business banking and cash management.

Our platform combines AI agents, automated financial workflows, and integrated financial products that save brands time and money.

Customers have generated billions in sales and include well-known brands such as Cuts https://www.cutsclothing.com/, Tushy https://hellotushy.com/, NYON https://newyorkornowhere.com/, Sabah https://www.sabah.am/, Still Here https://www.stillhere.nyc/, Alice Mushrooms https://alicemushrooms.com/, Original Grain https://www.originalgrain.com/, birddogs https://www.birddogs.com/, and more.

Our team includes alumni of Shopify, Square, Toast, Rippling, and McKinsey.

We’ve raised $42M in equity from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/962259dc-2fcd-40ed-8403-289c8a933cc7/image.png]



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/4192f743-4861-44cd-a1f6-473b86c927ae/join%20us%20(1).png]




ABOUT THIS ROLE



We’re hiring a Senior Product Manager to own the Intelligence product surface at Highbeam across banking, bill pay, cards, and treasury. You’ll define how AI shows up in financial workflows, ship core agentic workflows (automated AP, treasury optimization, anomaly detection), and drive weekly product velocity from spec to launch. The bar is a meaningful product shipped fast, with adoption and monetization to match.

This role is based in our NoHo, NYC office.



What you’ll do

 - Own the Intelligence product surface across banking, bill pay, cards, and treasury

 - Define how AI shows up in financial workflows (automation vs control, trust, explainability)

 - Ship core agentic workflows (e.g., automated AP, treasury optimization, anomaly detection)

 - Drive weekly product velocity: specs → build → QA → launch

 - Run continuous customer insight loops and translate them into product decisions

 - Own 0→1 product definition in ambiguous areas

 - Drive adoption and monetization, not just launches: ensure workflows are actually used

 - Improve speed of iteration across product (faster cycles, tighter feedback loops)

 - Contribute to product narrative + positioning



Net impact

 - Highbeam becomes known for AI-native financial workflows, not just banking

 - Faster shipping of meaningful product (not incremental features)

 - Measurable lift in product adoption (bill pay, cards, treasury)



Qualifications

 - 4-6 years PM experience; early-stage operator (PM #1–5 at Series A–C)

 - Comfortable with AI tools for iteration and product validation

 - Has built AI/LLM products as the core experience

 - Strong product builder: writes specs, QA’s builds, ships end-to-end

 - High velocity, comfortable shipping weekly in ambiguous environments

 - Deep customer development instincts (regular user conversations, synthesis)

 - Strong product taste, especially in workflow + UX simplicity

 - Background in fintech or vertical SaaS (Ramp, Brex, Mercury, etc.)

 - Bonus: engineering background



You'll thrive at Highbeam if...

 - You love working in-person with a high-performing team

 - You enjoy working in an idea-meritocratic, low-ego environment

 - You are proactive and self-directed, and you excel in ambiguous, fast moving environments

 - You care about delivering a polished customer experience

 - Bonus: You have experience working in fintech, AI or early-stage startups



What we offer

 - Chance to join the founding team of a well-funded startup chasing a huge opportunity

 - Competitive salary and meaningful equity

 - Great location: Our NYC office is in NoHo, a short walk from the A,C,E · B,D,F,M · N,Q,R,W · 1 · 6 trains. Our Toronto office is downtown near St. Andrew Station.

 - Generous PTO

 - Comprehensive health & benefits package

 - Stipends for lunch, commute, wellness, and purchasing customer products

 - Yearly offsite—last year we went to Mexico City!

source_jobs.title: People and Talent Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
Highbeam is building the future of business banking and cash management.

Our platform combines AI agents, automated financial workflows, and integrated financial products that save brands time and money.

Customers have generated billions in sales and include well-known brands such as Cuts https://www.cutsclothing.com/, Tushy https://hellotushy.com/, NYON https://newyorkornowhere.com/, Sabah https://www.sabah.am/, Still Here https://www.stillhere.nyc/, Alice Mushrooms https://alicemushrooms.com/, Original Grain https://www.originalgrain.com/, birddogs https://www.birddogs.com/, and more.

Our team includes alumni of Shopify, Square, Toast, Rippling, and McKinsey.

We’ve raised $42M in equity from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/962259dc-2fcd-40ed-8403-289c8a933cc7/image.png]



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/4192f743-4861-44cd-a1f6-473b86c927ae/join%20us%20(1).png]




ABOUT THIS ROLE



We're looking for a People & Talent Operations Manager to own the full people function at Highbeam. This is a hands-on role: you’ll run the day-to-day across people operations, recruiting coordination, payroll, and compliance, refining the processes and controls we already have in place and bringing more rigor to them as the team grows. You’ll help sharpen the policies that govern how we run by enforcing and embedding them into our internal AI agents, flag exceptions, and surface issues before they hit the team.

This role is based in our Toronto office.




WHAT YOU’LL DO



PEOPLE OPERATIONS

 - Run payroll, contractor payments, and bonus cycles across the US and Canada, sharpening the cycles we already operate

 - Own employee onboarding end-to-end: offer letters, I-9s, payroll setup, and option grant coordination with outside counsel

 - Manage employee lifecycle transitions (onboarding, role changes, offboarding) with care and professionalism

 - Administer 401(k), parental leave, and other benefits programs, refining enrollment and rollover workflows as we grow

 - Review and process employee expenses in line with company policy, tightening controls where they’re loose

 - Maintain and iterate on HR policies and employment contracts as the team scales

 - Stay ahead of state, federal, and tax compliance (multi-state payroll, business registrations, insurance), modernizing the controls that protect the company


RECRUITING

 - Own the candidate experience from first touch to offer, ensuring every interaction reflects the Highbeam brand

 - Own the ATS: maintain job postings, manage candidate pipelines with hiring managers, and keep hiring moving efficiently

 - Coordinate recruiting logistics including onsite schedules, candidate communications, and follow-up with external recruiters

 - Manage agency relationships, including contracts and payments

 - Source candidates directly, building out our pipeline for key roles



Qualifications

 - 3 - 5 years in people operations, HR, business operations, or a related cross-functional role, ideally at a startup or high-growth company

 - Experience running multi-jurisdiction payroll (US and Canada for us)

 - Comfortable working with employment lawyers, payroll tools, and ATS tools (Ashby a plus)

 - High attention to detail and discretion with sensitive information; pragmatic about when to keep things simple

 - Self-directed; you can identify what needs to get done without being told

 - Excellent communication skills, especially around sensitive topics like compensation and compliance

 - Experience supporting H-1B or other immigration processes is a plus

 - Familiarity with Canadian employment law and ROE filings is a bonus

 - Interest in using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, or similar) to encode policies, build agentic workflows, and automate operational work is a plus



You'll thrive at Highbeam if...

 - You love working in-per

source_jobs.title: Senior Technical Program Manager
source_jobs.description:
Highbeam is building the future of business banking and cash management.

Our platform combines AI agents, automated financial workflows, and integrated financial products that save brands time and money.

Customers have generated billions in sales and include well-known brands such as Cuts https://www.cutsclothing.com/, Tushy https://hellotushy.com/, NYON https://newyorkornowhere.com/, Sabah https://www.sabah.am/, Still Here https://www.stillhere.nyc/, Alice Mushrooms https://alicemushrooms.com/, Original Grain https://www.originalgrain.com/, birddogs https://www.birddogs.com/, and more.

Our team includes alumni of Shopify, Square, Toast, Rippling, and McKinsey.

We’ve raised $42M in equity from Acrew, FirstMark, Mayfield, and Two Sigma Ventures.



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/962259dc-2fcd-40ed-8403-289c8a933cc7/image.png]



[https://app.ashbyhq.com/api/images/user-content/7f0a2d9f-58d8-4b30-b1d9-7e652dfbd66d/4192f743-4861-44cd-a1f6-473b86c927ae/join%20us%20(1).png]




ABOUT THIS ROLE



We’re hiring a Senior Technical Program Manager to drive execution across the most critical partner programs at Highbeam. You’ll own timelines, dependencies, and delivery across banking partners, card and payment vendors, and compliance workstreams (SOC2, BSA/AML, PCI), translating between compliance, product, engineering, and external partners. The bar is external programs that ship faster and more predictably, with leadership no longer pulled into day-to-day coordination.

This role is based in our NoHo, NYC office or Toronto office.



What you’ll do

 - Drive execution across partners like Unit, bank partners, card and payment vendors

 - Own the critical path for partner and compliance work, freeing leadership to focus on broader priorities

 - Own timelines, dependencies, and delivery for cross-functional programs

 - Translate between compliance, product, engineering, and partners

 - Drive pay-by-card, treasury, onboarding, and any future partner integration coordination

 - Own compliance and audit readiness (SOC2, BSA/AML, PCI)

 - Establish scalable processes for operating in regulated systems

 - Unblock engineering from external dependencies and ambiguity

 - Lead incident response and resolution across money movement systems

 - Ensure partner requirements are clearly defined, tracked, and delivered

 - Create clear, trusted visibility into program status for leadership

 - Hold vendors and partners accountable to timelines and outcomes



Net impact

 - External programs ship faster and more predictably

 - Engineering velocity increases due to fewer external blockers

 - Reduced operational and compliance risk

 - Leadership is no longer pulled into day-to-day coordination

 - Highbeam can scale into more complex financial workflows with confidence



Qualifications

 - 5-8 years TPM or technical program leadership experience, ideally in fintech, payments, or other regulated industries

 - Track record managing partner integrations with banks, card networks, or payment vendors (Unit, Galileo, Stripe Issuing, etc.)

 - Deep familiarity with compliance frameworks (SOC2, BSA/AML, PCI) and how they map to engineering work

 - Strong systems thinking; can translate between compliance, product, engineering, and external partners

 - Comfortable shipping in ambiguity, with a bias for driving clarity and removing blockers

 - Experience leading incident response in money movement systems or other production-critical environments

 - Skilled at holding vendors and partners accountable to timelines and outcomes

 - Strong written communication; can produce clear, trusted program status for leadership

 - Comfortable with AI tools to accelerate program coordination and documentation

 - Bonus: engineering background or hands-on technical depth



You'll thrive at Highbeam if...

 - You love working in-person with a high-performing team

 - You enjoy working in an idea-meritocr

notes: Highbeam is a fintech startup with a strong focus on AI and automation.
